title: Rupert Sheldrake discusses the retrocausal nature of the mind.
summary: Rupert Sheldrake discusses philosopher Alfred North Whitehead's theory that mental causation operates retrocausally—from future toward past—while physical causation works past to future. He explains how consciousness deals with possibilities that collapse into physical facts when we make choices, similar to quantum wave function collapse.

key_claims.claim: Alfred North Whitehead believed that all mental causation is retrocausal, operating from future towards past while physical causation operates from past towards future
Conscious minds are primarily concerned with possibilities rather than actualities
When we choose one possibility from many, it collapses like a quantum wave function and becomes a physical fact
